reasoning: The base rate for a successful appeal of a CAS decision to the Swiss Federal Tribunal (SFT) is exceptionally low (below 1%). The SFT does not typically admit new evidence or re-evaluate facts, but rather intervenes only in cases of severe procedural flaws (such as breaches of the right to be heard or public policy violations). The community prediction for this question is currently at 0.008 (0.8%). The burden of proof for the U.S. side to demonstrate a critical procedural failure by the CAS, rather than just introducing new time-stamped video evidence that the CAS previously refused to review, is huge. It is highly unlikely this decision will be overturned before 2026.
